title: github jcob-bd/notebooklm-mcp-cliを調べて、kernel/FUGUEに活用できるか検証せよ。/kernelで。
summary_text: Findings from the two files: - [test-notebooklm-cli-adapter.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/tests/test-notebooklm-cli-adapter.sh) is good for adapter behavior (`resolve-only`, approval gate, receipt write, smoke), but it does not cover the upcoming `skill/profile/sync` layer at all. - [sync-googleworkspace-skills.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/scripts/skills/sync-googleworkspace-skills.sh) gives a solid baseline pattern for a NotebookLM sync script (manifest-driven selection, managed marker, dry-run, force semantics, payload validation). Minimal test set I recommend for new NotebookLM sync/profile layer: 1. `manifest_required_only` - Input manifest with `required` + `optional`. - Run sync without `--with-optional`. - Assert only required skills are processed. 2. `manifest_with_optional` - Same fixture, run with `--with-optional`. - Assert both required and optional entries are processed. 3. `target_routing` - Run with `--target codex`, `--target claude`, `--target both`. - Assert install paths are written only to expected target dirs. 4. `dry_run_no_writes` - Run with `--dry-run`. - Assert no directories/files created and no managed marker written. 5. `managed_vs_unmanaged_replace` - Existing target dir with marker: should replace. - Existing target dir without marker: should skip unless `--force`. - With `--force`, should replace. 6. `payload_validation_frontmatter` - Fixture skill missing `SKILL.md`, missing `name`, or missing `description`. - Assert sync fails with clear error. 7. `security_guardrail_flags` - Fixture `SKILL.md` containing forbidden flags (e.g. `--yolo`, `--full-auto`). - Assert sync fails. 8. `profile_manifest_drift_check` (doc/profile layer) - Validate that `docs/notebooklm-skills-profile.md` listed baseline skills exactly match `config/skills/notebooklm-cli-baseline.tsv` required set. - Fail on mismatch. Risk notes: - Highest risk: network-coupled tests. `sync-googleworkspace-skills.sh` downloads snapshots; NotebookLM sync tests should avoid network by injecting a local snapshot fixture path. - `rm -rf` replacement path should be guarded by tests to avoid accidental broad deletion if target path variables are empty/mis-set. - Current frontmatter checks are regex-based and shallow; acceptable for minimum viability, but add strict parser later if false positives appear. - Existing adapter tests cover `visual-brief` path well; add one `slide-prep` receipt assertion when skill layer starts consuming that action to avoid regression gap.
